title: Fixes #15206 Correctly handle ssh to machine when VM found for WSL
body:
**Which issue(s) this PR fixes:**
Fixes #15206

**What this PR does / why we need it:**
On Windows the VM is not correctly identified when the connection is not
default. The load VM by name passes, but wants to try a remote
connection which results in a no service configured.

This change will not check the `validVM` flag but instead if the load
returned a valid entry.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Gerard Braad <me@gbraad.nl>


**Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?**
```release-note
 podman machine ssh correctly identifies the default VM
```

@Luap99 There is a definitely a problem in this code; behaviour is different between the providers.

```go
	vm, err = provider.LoadVMByName(vmName)
```

`loadByVMName` didn't return `nil` but a valid value. Originally I had:

```go
if (err == nil) {  // vm != nil
  validVM = true;
}
```

as I believe this is what the result of the test should be, as this state isn't set or otherwise used after the load.
Somehow the Linux implementation regards this as a valid 'remote connection' ?

I can't verify on Linux/macOS myself now, so I can't compare the differences (on PTO and my remote machine is unreachable now).
Hmm, let me take a look and see if I can help track it down.

@n1hility did you see this?
Hope you can repro the error and share your thoughts
@gbraad it seems to work for me. Perhaps already fixed? Any tips on how to reproduce? (although there is a regression in the main branch atm from some in-flight ssh client work)
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@gbraad @Luap99 @n1hility What is going on with this PR?
Over two months without any activity.  Should we close?
Closing as the PR has stalled.  Feel free to re-open if you find time to get back to it.
